retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Second Generation
retrieved_docs.content: In this type of cement, the polyacid was already added to the powder, and the setting was achieved by mixing the powder with water or a tartaric acid solution. This GIC is known as water-hardening cement. The main advantages of the second-generation system are enhanced shelf life, decreased initial viscosity, and improved strength.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Physical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: The thermal expansion coefficient of GIC is similar to enamel and dentin. The thermal diffusivity of GIC is similar to dentin, providing thermal insulation to the pulp. The solubility of GIC in water is higher than zinc phosphate and zinc polycarboxylate. [14] GICs are tooth-colored restorative materials but are not recommended to restore anterior teeth because of their opacity and lack of translucency.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: The setting of GIC is through an acid-base reaction involving the following stages: Dissolution and decomposition: after mixing the powder and liquid, the acid dissolves the surface of the glass particles to release SiO 4 4- , Ca 2+ , Na + , and F - ions. Initial setting: the Ca 2+ released in the aqueous medium reacts with polyacrylic acid to form a 3-dimensional cross-linked structure. At this time, the material has low strength, and it can be carved using sharp instruments. Final setting: the setting reaction continues during the next 24 hours. During this time, SiO 4 4- forms the silica gel. The slow-moving Al 3+ ions enter the aqueous medium and cross-link the polyacrylic acid chains, displacing the Ca 2+ ions. This increases the final strength of the set cement. Maturation: the aluminum and calcium cross-linked polyacrylate chains get hydrated over time by absorbing water from the aqueous medium. This process is known as maturation. [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Impression Materials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: 4. Setting reaction: It sets in 2 steps: first, by the hydrolysis of ZnO to form zinc hydroxide (water is required to initiate the reaction). The second stage is a typical acid-base reaction in which zinc hydroxide reacts with eugenolic acid to form zinc eugenolate, forming a chelate and water as a byproduct. The final set structure consists of ZnO particles embedded in the matrix of zinc eugenolate. The impression paste's setting time can be altered in a clinical setup according to need, either decreased by adding a drop of water during mixing or increased by adding vaseline during mixing. The material has a good flow, which records fine details. ZOE's impression has excellent dimensional stability for 24 hrs; therefore, it can be poured within this time. However, the special impression tray underneath should not deform. [11] 5. Indications:

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement has compressive strength equivalent to zinc phosphate cement, whereas its tensile strength is slightly higher than zinc phosphate cement. Type I GIC has lower compressive strength than Type II GIC. GIC is less stiff and has a lower modulus of elasticity comparable to dentin but lesser than zinc phosphate cement. [8] The resilience of GIC is similar to dentin, which is why it is known as man-made dentin.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The longevity of glass ionomer cement restorations is affected by occlusal forces, porosities in set cement, desiccation or water absorption during initial setting, and use of the mixed cement after the loss of gloss. Furthermore, larger GIC particles decrease the wear rate; the cement's solubility depends on the amount of cement at the margins.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Dispersed phase glasses: using dispersed phases of strengthening crystallites like aluminum oxide, titanium oxide, and zirconium oxide improved the strength. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: The powder-to-liquid ratio determines the mechanical properties of the set cement. More powder increases the consistency of the cement, strength, and speed of the setting reaction. However, the powder-to-liquid ratio can be increased until a critical point. The volume of the physical matrix will be deficient in binding and holding the cement ingredients together; the mechanical properties will be compromised significantly. Thus, the powder-to-liquid ratio should strictly comply with the manufacturer's instructions. Using pre-graduated self-contained mixing capsules solves this problem. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Cermet ionomer cement: manufactured by sintering the metal and glass powders together, which helps achieve a strong metal bond to the glass. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: Setting occurs by light-induced polymerization. The acid-base setting mechanism does not exist due to the absence of polyacids from the composition.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- The Role of Water in the Setting of GIC
retrieved_docs.content: GIC is water-based cement because water constitutes 11% to 24% of the set cement. Water plays a crucial role in the GIC setting. In the initial phases, it acts as a reaction medium. In later stages, it mediates the slow hydration of the cross-linked chains.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Structure of Set Cement
retrieved_docs.content: The cement consists of a core and matrix. The core constitutes unmixed powder particles, whereas the matrix comprises polyacid and HEMA polymer linked with hydrogen bonding.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Composition
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement is generally supplied as a powder-liquid system that is mixed manually. The powder is fluoro-aluminosilicate glass, and the liquid is an aqueous solution of polyacrylic acid. Polyacrylic acid is copolymerized with carboxylic acid, maleic, tartaric, and itaconic acid to regulate the viscosity and stabilize the liquid. The liquid exhibits a thixotropic behavior: its thickness can be reversed by shaking or warming the bottle. Other supply options include capsules, twin syringes, and single-bottle water-settable form (freeze-dried polyacrylic acid added to GIC powder). [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- First Generation
retrieved_docs.content: This material was developed because silicate cement was showing poor clinical performance. To overcome this problem, the alumina-to-silica ratio was increased. The first glass ionomer developed was ASPA I (aluminosilicate polyacrylic acid), which had a sluggish setting, was sensitive to moisture while sitting, and had poor esthetics, resulting in limited clinical utility. Wilson and Crisp added d-tartaric acid to the liquid, allowing the use of glasses containing lesser fluoride, which is more translucent. ASPA I was then named ASPA II and accounted for the first glass ionomer cement used for clinical applications. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- The Role of Water in the Setting of GIC
retrieved_docs.content: The water can evaporate quickly during the initial phases due to exposure to ambient air. On the other hand, water contamination during this stage can cause the matrix's dissolution and calcium ions' leakage. [7] Thus, uptake or loss of water results in a weak, opaque, and more soluble cement. The cement's uptake or loss of free water can be prevented by coating the surface of the restoration with varnish, cocoa butter, or petroleum jelly immediately after placing the restoration.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 96
retrieved_docs.content: ammonium chloride, calcium, potassium, selenium, zinc, manganese, iron and other minerals—are organized within overlapping pharmacological and chemical classifications, how specific contraindications (e.g., bismuth subcarbonate) must be considered, and how they contribute to comprehensive patient care through mineral supplementation, electrolyte management, targeted diuretic therapy and supportive measures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1985
retrieved_docs.content: Zinc supplements are classified as part of zinc gluconate, indicating that zinc gluconate represents a specific formulation within the broader category of zinc supplements.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2449
retrieved_docs.content: Sodium phosphate is classified among the drugs used for treatment of hypercalcemia, indicating its role in managing elevated calcium levels.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1902
retrieved_docs.content: Zinc Supplements are directly associated with zinc (zinek), indicating that they serve as a source of this essential mineral, and they are classified as part of the zinc gluconate group, meaning that zinc gluconate represents a specific formulation within the broader category of zinc supplements. This relationship underscores that zinc supplements encompass various zinc-containing compounds, with zinc gluconate being a common and representative example used to deliver elemental zinc for nutritional and therapeutic purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2848
retrieved_docs.content: Replacement preparation includes dibasic potassium phosphate and sodium hyaluronate as its constituent components, indicating that both dibasic potassium phosphate and sodium hyaluronate are child elements of the replacement preparation formulation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 1752
retrieved_docs.content: Magnesium gluconate is contraindicated in patients with قصور الكلى (renal failure), meaning its use should be avoided in individuals suffering from kidney failure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 2379
retrieved_docs.content: The VITAMIN B COMPLEX with iron and zinc tablet includes zinc as one of its ingredients, indicating that zinc is a component of this multivitamin formulation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 1 -- Community 45
retrieved_docs.content: distinct physiological actions—antioxidant protection, metabolic and enzymatic support, and phosphate‑chelating calcium provision—while being integrated within broader multinutrient formulations to meet diverse patient‑care needs.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 2353
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium protein preparations and calcium lactate are both classified as mineral supplements, representing specific types of calcium‑containing mineral supplement products used to support nutritional needs.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1981
retrieved_docs.content: Magnesium citrate is contraindicated in patients who have an intestinal perforation, as its use in the presence of this condition is not recommended.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 1198
retrieved_docs.content: Nasogastric tube procedures classify the nasogastric tube, indicating that the range of clinical actions, techniques, and protocols are organized around the use, insertion, management, and care of the nasogastric tube.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 1 -- Community 148
retrieved_docs.content: activity, phosphate chelation, inhibition of bone resorption) and their synergistic inclusion with iron, calcium, other vitamins and trace minerals underpin their therapeutic, preventive and formulation‑stabilizing roles in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2889
retrieved_docs.content: Chromic chloride is classified within the category of electrolyte/mineral replacement, indicating that it functions as a mineral or electrolyte supplement used in therapeutic replacement regimens.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 2103
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium acetate is classified as a calcium compound, reflecting an “isa” relationship that places calcium acetate within the broader category of calcium compounds. This categorization indicates that calcium acetate shares the chemical characteristics of calcium compounds and informs its use in medical applications, such as serving as a phosphate binder in patients with renal impairment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 4 -- Community 2788
retrieved_docs.content: Replacement preparation is classified as a potassium supplement and includes calcium lactate and dibasic potassium phosphate as its constituent components, indicating that this formulation belongs to the broader category of potassium supplements while being composed of those specific ingredients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 578
retrieved_docs.content: and calcium pantothenate are common, active constituents of comprehensive micronutrient preparations, contribute to essential metabolic pathways, and must be considered in the context of specific contraindications such as renal calculi.
